tools.name: write_readme
tools.title: Write README
tools.description: Generates a README.md file for a single file or an entire project folder by analyzing the source code and writing human-friendly documentation.

parameters.folder_name: str - name of the local workspace folder or file to document
parameters.extension: List[str] - file extensions to include while scanning, e.g. ['.py', '.js']

example_goals: write a readme for my_project folder
generate documentation for this repo
create a README.md for the backend folder
document my python files
create readme.md

tools.name: write_requirements
tools.title: Write Requirements
tools.description: Scans a file or folder, detects the imported/used libraries, and generates a requirements.txt (or equivalent dependency file) for the project.

parameters.folder_name: str - name of the local workspace folder or file to scan
parameters.extension: List[str] - file extensions to scan, e.g. ['.py']

example_goals: create requirements.txt for my_project
list all dependencies used in this folder
generate a requirements file for these python scripts

tools.name: push_folder
tools.title: Push Folder to GitHub
tools.description: Pushes a local workspace folder to a GitHub repository, creating an initial commit with the given message.

parameters.workspace_folder_name: str - local folder inside workspace to push
parameters.commit: str = 'initial commit via Agent' - commit message

example_goals: push my_project folder to github repo test-repo
upload this folder to my github
commit and push the workspace folder to repo 'demo'

tools.name: pull_repo
tools.title: Pull Repository
tools.description: Clones/pulls a GitHub repository (specific branch) into a local folder path inside the workspace.
example_goals: pull https://github.com/user/repo into local folder repo1
clone this repository to my workspace
download the dev branch of a repo

tools.name: delete_repo
tools.title: Delete Repository
tools.description: Permanently deletes a GitHub repository owned by the authenticated user.

parameters.repo_name: str - name of the repository to delete

example_goals: delete repo test9
remove my old-project repository from github

tools.name: create_repo
tools.title: Create Repository
tools.description: Creates a new GitHub repository, optionally private, with a description.

parameters.repo_name: str - name of the new repository
parameters.private: bool = False - whether the repo should be private
parameters.description: str = 'Handled via GitHub Automation Agent' - repository description

example_goals: create repo 'test9'
create a private repository named secrets-vault
make a new github repo for my project

tools.name: list_repos
tools.title: List Repositories
tools.description: Lists all GitHub repositories belonging to the authenticated user.
example_goals: list all my github repos
show me my repositories
what repos do i have on github

tools.name: list_repo_files
tools.title: List Repository Files
tools.description: Lists all files inside a specific GitHub repository, optionally scoped to a sub-folder.

parameters.repo_name: str - repository to list files from
parameters.sub_folder_name: str = '' - optional sub folder path inside the repo

example_goals: list all files in repo test9
show me the files inside the src folder of my-repo
what files are in this repository
